metadata.title: LipSync Generator - Create Perfect AI Lip-Sync Videos
metadata.description: Upload video and audio to create natural AI lip-sync videos. Match speech to faces for talking-head clips, dubbing, localization, and social content.

workspace.title: LipSync Generator
workspace.description: Upload face media and audio, then create a talking video concept with natural mouth timing, facial expression, and clean creator-video composition.
workspace.defaultPrompt: Create a natural lip-sync video from this speaker video and audio. Keep facial identity stable, match mouth shapes to speech timing, preserve realistic expression, and maintain clean studio lighting.

uploadSlots.label: Upload face video or image
uploadSlots.description: Use a clear front-facing speaker clip or portrait with visible mouth and stable lighting.

uploadSlots.label: Upload reference audio
uploadSlots.description: MP3, WAV, M4A, AAC, OGG, or WEBM audio with clean speech works best.

workspace.readyText: Your face media, audio, and lip-sync settings are ready.

showcase.title: Lip-sync video examples
showcase.description: Use these prompt patterns for talking portraits, dubbing, explainers, character clips, and creator videos.

items.title: Talking Founder
items.description: Clean speech timing for a business portrait.
items.prompt: Sync this founder video to the uploaded audio, preserve natural mouth movement, slight head motion, realistic expression, clean studio background.

items.title: Product Explainer
items.prompt: Create a lip-synced product explainer, accurate mouth timing, confident facial expression, bright office lighting, natural presenter posture.

items.title: Talking Photo
items.description: Animate a portrait from uploaded speech.
items.prompt: Turn this portrait into a talking video, match lips to uploaded audio, keep identity stable, subtle blinking, natural face movement.

items.title: Multilingual Dub
items.prompt: Lip-sync this speaker to the new language audio, preserve emotional tone, mouth shapes, and original camera framing.

what.title: AI lip-sync clips from face media and clean speech
what.description: People searching for an AI lip sync generator want a face, an audio track, and believable mouth timing. This page explains the practical requirements behind that workflow: visible lips, clean speech, stable identity, and review before publishing.

items.title: Talking videos
items.description: Sync a speaker face to uploaded audio for explainers, ads, creator clips, spokesperson videos, or updated scripts when the source shot is still useful.

items.title: Talking photos
items.description: Animate a still portrait when you do not have source footage, while using a clear front-facing image so the mouth and face can move naturally.

items.title: Dubbing workflows
items.description: Replace or localize speech while keeping the same face, framing, and visual identity, which is useful for multilingual clips and revised voiceovers.

how.title: How to create AI lip-sync videos
how.description: Lip-sync quality depends less on a long prompt and more on source quality. Clear mouth visibility, clean audio, and realistic expression cues produce more trustworthy talking videos.

steps.title: Upload face media
steps.description: Choose a front-facing video or portrait where the mouth, jaw, and cheeks are visible. Avoid hands, microphones, masks, heavy blur, or extreme side angles.

steps.title: Add speech audio
steps.description: Use clean speech with limited background noise, clear pauses, and stable volume so the generated mouth shapes can align with the spoken words.

steps.title: Generate and review timing
steps.description: Check mouth closure, jaw movement, expression, speaker identity, and timing before exporting. Small timing errors are easier to catch before the clip is captioned or published.

features.title: Controls that affect lip-sync believability
features.description: A helpful lip-sync page should make the quality factors clear. Users need to know what they can control, what the model infers, and which source problems usually create distracting results.

items.title: Audio upload
items.description: Upload speech, voiceover, dialogue, or a short song section when you have the rights to use it and the voice is clean enough for timing.

items.title: Image or video input
items.description: Create talking-photo clips from portraits or re-sync an existing speaker video depending on whether you need a new host or an updated take.

items.title: Dubbing support
items.description: Guide voice replacement, multilingual dubbing, and localized talking videos while keeping mouth motion, expression, and identity consistent.

items.title: Resolution options
items.description: Prepare quick drafts for timing review or higher-quality exports for ads, lessons, explainers, and polished creator clips.

useCases.title: Best uses for AI lip sync
useCases.description: Lip-sync generation is useful when speech needs to match a face without frame-by-frame editing, but the best use cases still depend on consent, clear source media, and review of the final talking clip.

items.title: Marketing videos
items.description: Create spokesperson clips, product explainers, UGC-style ads, and revised campaign lines when reshooting the speaker is slower than updating the audio.

items.title: Localization
items.description: Dub videos into other languages while keeping the same speaker shot, then review pronunciation, mouth timing, and cultural context before publishing.

items.title: Talking avatars
items.description: Animate portraits, character faces, education hosts, or brand avatars from audio when a still image is enough for the communication goal.

items.title: Content repair
items.description: Replace unclear audio, update a line, test a new voiceover, or repair a short section without reshooting the entire talking-head clip.

value.title: Why lip-sync quality depends on inputs
value.description: AI lip sync aligns mouth shape, jaw movement, and expression to speech. Clean source media and clean audio are the trust signals users need before they decide whether a result is believable.

points.title: Visible mouth detail
points.description: The model needs a clear view of lips, teeth, jaw, and nearby facial muscles to build natural speech movement instead of a floating or stiff mouth.

points.title: Clean audio timing
points.description: Speech without heavy music, crowd noise, or overlapping voices gives the system cleaner timing cues and makes review easier for the creator.

points.title: Stable identity
points.description: Stable framing, consistent lighting, and a clear face help preserve identity across the generated clip and reduce distracting visual drift.

related.title: More talking-video workflows
related.description: Continue from lip-sync into baby podcast videos, cinematic scenes, or short-form motion concepts.

faq.title: AI Lip Sync FAQ
faq.description: Answers for users searching how audio-to-video lip sync works, what files to upload, and how to improve results.

items.question: What is an AI lip sync generator?
items.answer: An AI lip sync generator analyzes a face in an uploaded video or image and aligns mouth movement, jaw motion, and expression with uploaded audio or generated speech.

items.question: Can I use both video and image input?
items.answer: Yes. Video input is useful when you already have a speaker clip, while image input can create a talking-photo style result from a still portrait.

items.question: What audio files work best?
items.answer: Clean speech audio works best. Use MP3, WAV, M4A, AAC, OGG, or WEBM where the voice is clear and not buried under music, echo, or sound effects.

items.question: Can AI lip sync handle multiple languages?
items.answer: AI lip-sync workflows can be used for dubbing and localization. For best results, use clear translated audio and keep the emotional tone close to the original scene.

items.question: How do I avoid unnatural mouth movement?
items.answer: Use a front-facing face, avoid blocked mouths, keep the head stable, upload clean audio, and avoid prompts that request exaggerated expression changes.

items.question: Can I lip-sync a photo?
items.answer: Yes. A talking-photo workflow animates a still image to match the audio, but results usually look best when the portrait is sharp, front-facing, and naturally lit.

items.question: Can I use lip-sync videos commercially?
items.answer: Commercial use depends on the rights to the face, voice, script, music, and uploaded footage. Get consent before using another person's likeness or voice in generated content.

items.question: Is AI lip sync the same as deepfake video?
items.answer: Lip sync focuses on matching speech timing to a face, but it can still involve likeness and consent risks. Use it transparently and avoid impersonation or misleading content.

finalCta.title: Create an AI lip-sync video
finalCta.description: Upload face media, add clean speech audio, and prepare a talking clip with natural mouth timing and stable identity.
